hero.title: The Global Standard for
hero.description: The Agentic Commerce Framework® (ACF) is the definitive governance methodology for deploying, supervising, and controlling autonomous agentic systems in commercial environments.

principles.title: 4 Founding Principles
principles.subtitle: Four immutable axioms defining the boundary between human authority and autonomous agent execution.

principle1.title: Decision Sovereignty
principle1.description: Critical strategic decisions are never delegated to autonomous agents.

principle2.title: Governance by Design
principle2.description: Governance frameworks are defined before deployment, not after.

principle3.title: Ultimate Human Control
principle3.description: Every agentic system must preserve the ability for human intervention.

principle4.title: Traceable Accountability
principle4.description: Every autonomous action must be auditable and attributable.

layers.title: 4 Operational Layers
layers.subtitle: A hierarchical architecture from strategic governance to real-time execution supervision.

layer1.title: Governance & Sovereignty
layer1.description: Sovereignty charter, governance committee, RACI matrix, non-delegable zone map.

layer2.title: Decision Policy
layer2.description: Weighted objectives, arbitration rules, escalation thresholds, regulatory constraints.

layer3.title: Agent System
layer3.description: Explicit mandate per agent, interaction perimeter, autonomy level, 5-category taxonomy.

layer4.title: Execution & Supervision
layer4.description: Adaptive gating matrix, multi-level alerts, 18 sovereignty KPIs, live dashboards.

maturity.title: 4 Agentic Maturity Levels
maturity.subtitle: ACF classifies systems by autonomy level. Level 2 is the recommended deployment target.

level0.name: Classical Automation
level0.description: Fixed rules, no ML. Human intervention for any modification.

level1.name: Assisted Agents
level1.description: Agents analyze and recommend. Every final decision remains with a human.

level2.name: Governed Agents
level2.description: Agents decide within strict governance. Non-delegable zones locked.

level3.name: Supervised Autonomous
level3.description: Agents decide and learn. Maximum governance. For mature organizations only.

modules.title: 8 Implementation Modules
modules.subtitle: A sequential path deployed progressively over 6–18 months. Each module builds on the previous.

module1.name: Sovereignty Diagnostic
module1.description: Sovereignty Score calculation. Risk zone mapping.

module5.name: Security & Reversibility
module5.description: Sandboxing, reversibility plan. Emergency stop design.

video.title:
Governance Cannot Wait.
Agents Already Decide.
video.description1: In 2026, autonomous agents are already executing decisions in commercial environments — setting prices, qualifying leads, processing claims, managing contracts. Most organizations have no framework to govern them.
video.description2: ACF was created to fill that void — before a governance failure becomes a legal, financial, or reputational crisis.

quote.text: The question is no longer whether to deploy agents. It is how to deploy them without surrendering your sovereignty.
quote.author: — Agentic Commerce Framework®

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the Agentic Commerce Framework® and AI governance.

q1.question: What is the Agentic Commerce Framework® (ACF)?
q1.answer: The ACF is the global governance standard for organizations deploying autonomous AI agents in commercial environments. It defines 4 founding principles, 4 operational layers, 8 implementation modules, and 18 sovereignty KPIs to ensure that humans retain strategic control over agentic systems.

q2.question: Who created the ACF Standard?
q2.answer: The ACF was created by Vincent DORANGE, an AI governance expert. The framework was developed to address the growing need for structured governance as autonomous agents increasingly make decisions in commerce, finance, and operations.

q3.question: What are the 4 Founding Principles of ACF?
q3.answer: The four immutable principles are: (1) Decision Sovereignty — critical decisions are never delegated to agents, (2) Governance by Design — governance is defined before deployment, (3) Ultimate Human Control — every system preserves human intervention capability, (4) Traceable Accountability — every autonomous action is auditable and attributable.

q4.question: What is the ACF Sovereignty Score?
q4.answer: The ACF Score is a proprietary metric that measures your organization's decisional independence across 6 governance dimensions. It provides a composite sovereignty score, a 6-axis radar visualization, and a personalized action plan to strengthen governance over your agentic systems.

q5.question: What are the ACF maturity levels?
q5.answer: ACF defines 4 maturity levels: Level 0 (Classical Automation) — fixed rules, no ML; Level 1 (Assisted Agents) — agents recommend, humans decide; Level 2 (Governed Agents) — agents decide within strict governance, the recommended target; Level 3 (Supervised Autonomous) — agents decide and learn, requiring maximum governance.

q6.question: How does ACF relate to the EU AI Act?
q6.answer: The ACF framework is designed to be fully compatible with the EU AI Act. Its risk-based governance approach maps directly to the Act's requirements for high-risk AI systems. ACF Level 2 governance covers the key obligations for autonomous agents operating in commercial environments within the EU.

q7.question: What is the DDA (Delegated Decision Agent) role?
q7.answer: The DDA is a governance role defined by ACF. The Delegated Decision Agent Officer serves as the legal guardian of your autonomous agents — responsible for defining mandates, monitoring compliance, and ensuring that no agent exceeds its authorized decision perimeter.

q8.question: How do I get ACF certified?
q8.answer: ACF Certification is an independent attestation of compliance with the ACF governance standard. There are three certification paths (Level 1, 2, and 3), each requiring an audit process. Certified organizations receive a publicly verifiable badge with annual renewal and continuous monitoring.

q9.question: What is the ACF Emergency Stop Protocol?
q9.answer: The ACF Emergency Stop Protocol is a 3-level interrupt mechanism designed for autonomous agent systems. Level 1 pauses non-critical operations, Level 2 suspends all agent decision-making, and Level 3 performs a full system shutdown. Each level has defined response times and escalation procedures.

q10.question: Is ACF only for large enterprises?
q10.answer: No. ACF is designed to scale from startups deploying their first AI agent to large enterprises managing hundreds of autonomous systems. The 8 implementation modules can be deployed progressively over 6–18 months, allowing organizations of any size to build governance at their own pace.
